search_queries_used: site:who.int ICRC Basic Emergency Care reassessment observation disposition
site:rcem.ac.uk observation unit review emergency department guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic observation duration or discharge criteria
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: One condition-specific protocol cannot support a generic observation-unit review.
Duration and disposition remain clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children under ED observation; WHO BEC evidence is strongest for acute illness or trauma and is not a complete observation-unit standard.
setting_applicability: Emergency observation and reassessment before clinician disposition.
UAE_applicability: Reassessment domains transfer; UAE facility observation, monitoring, specialty, admission and discharge policies apply.
recency_verification: WHO/ICRC BEC exact reassessment and disposition material was re-reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: WHO/ICRC BEC remains the registered foundational source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact WHO/ICRC reassessment and disposition material supports interval history, change, current symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician risk context during observation only when reassessed; it does not imply reassessment occurred. The exact disposition material supports existing-result review, a clinician-entered plan, escalation, specialist discussion and handover only when performed or decided.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact secondary-survey and reassessment material supports measured vital data, manual examination, current severity, appearance and time-critical abnormalities only when reassessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.

unresolved_source_gaps: Reason for observation, required monitoring, treatment response, condition-specific review, pending results, elapsed time, diagnosis, plan and disposition criteria remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
